game_name: Dragon Quest V: Hand of the Heavenly Bride

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Established series mechanic from Dragon Quest I-IV
New to Dragon Quest V; monsters join after battles when party is strong enough, can be leveled like party members
Carried forward from Dragon Quest IV; allows party members to rest while exploring

element_introduction_order.element: Tactics system with individual AI control
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Expanded from Dragon Quest IV; allows setting AI routines per ally or manual control
Unique narrative element spanning ~30 years of hero's life; affects party composition and story branches
Primary movement mode; contains random encounters and NPCs
Series standard perspective for combat encounters
First Dragon Quest game to feature bonus dungeon; unlocked after beating main game

typical_tension_triggers: Random monster encounters during overworld travel
Boss battles (implied by party power requirements for monster recruitment)
Extended dungeon segments without wagon access
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Long-form narrative pacing spanning roughly 30 years of hero's life; alternates between exploration tension and narrative/relationship relief; monster recruitment provides persistent goal that extends mid-game engagement

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Monster recruitment conditions (party strength requirement); suggests hidden optional encounters and side objectives

difficulty_curve.early_game: Manageable; player is age 6, basic combat introduction with father Pankraz as initial party member
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating; 10-year timeskip removes starting companion, forces solo progression through enslavement period, gradually rebuild party with monster recruitment
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex; marriage choice opens branching scenarios; party composition becomes flexible via monster variety; bonus dungeon available for optional extreme challenge
notable_spikes: Father's death / shift to solo play after enslavement begins
Transition from small party to full monster roster management
Marriage choice and associated party restructuring

notable_level_patterns.description: Game divides into distinct narrative chapters tied to hero's age and life events (childhood with father, enslavement years, adulthood marriage, family formation). Each stage gates mechanics and party access differently, creating natural difficulty/content progression.

Monster recruitment gated by party strength requirement; creates implicit difficulty progression where player must reach certain power threshold before accessing new recruitable monsters, extending mid-game engagement and encouraging grinding/exploration.

Player selects marriage partner (minimum 2 options, 3 in DS remake), creating story branches that likely affect available companions, locations, or side content; delays endgame while providing replay value.

Party wagon allows rest stops during overworld exploration, creating safe zones that break exploration tension and provide narrative/character moments; spatial anchoring for party management decisions.
